assessment_factors.name: Customer Concentration Risk
assessment_factors.description: Degree of revenue dependency on top accounts and vulnerability to key account loss

assessment_factors.name: Competitive Displacement Exposure
assessment_factors.description: Vulnerability to competitive pressure from larger players offering price reductions
assessment_factors.scoring_criteria: 1-Minimal pressure, 3-Moderate pressure, 5-Severe pressure with active targeting

assessment_factors.name: Key Personnel Dependency
assessment_factors.description: Critical skill concentration and retention risk for Solutions Architects and senior staff

assessment_factors.name: Regulatory Compliance Complexity
assessment_factors.description: Multi-jurisdictional compliance burden across GDPR, PCI DSS, and sector regulations

assessment_factors.name: Technology Change Velocity
assessment_factors.description: Rate of technology change and automation adoption risk

assessment_factors.name: Integration Risk
assessment_factors.description: M&A integration complexity and post-acquisition execution risk

assessment_factors.name: Service Delivery Quality
assessment_factors.description: Client satisfaction, delivery SLA adherence, and quality metrics

assessment_factors.name: Financial Performance Stability
assessment_factors.description: Revenue growth trajectory, cash flow stability, and margin pressure

assessment_factors.name: Third-Party Risk Exposure
assessment_factors.description: Dependency on contractors, subcontractors, and technology vendors

assessment_factors.name: Data Security Posture
assessment_factors.description: Information security controls, incident history, and vulnerability management

risks.name: Customer Concentration and Key Account Loss
risks.description: Revenue dependency on major accounts creates vulnerability to competitive displacement, as demonstrated by Account A loss ($3.4M ARR) to Infosys in Q3. Top 10 accounts represent 42% of ARR.

risks.name: Competitive Pricing Pressure from Global SIs
risks.description: Infosys EMEA acquisition enables 28% price reductions and bundled services that Focus Group cannot match. Win rates declined 3pp and market share dropped 10bps in Q3.
risks.risk_tolerance: Win rate decline not exceeding 5pp annually

risks.name: Solutions Architect Attrition and Key Person Dependency
risks.description: Solutions Architect turnover at 12.8% creates delivery risk in talent-constrained market. Frankfurt practice lost key personnel to competitors, threatening DACH delivery capacity.

risks.name: Enterprise Renewal Performance Deterioration
risks.description: Q3 enterprise renewal cohort achieved only 88% vs 95% target. NRR fluctuating 103-105% below industry benchmarks, GRR at 91-92% indicates meaningful churn pressure.

risks.name: New Logo Acquisition Underperformance
risks.description: New logo acquisition at 82% of quota in Q1 indicating sales execution gaps. Financial services campaign required mid-quarter correction due to inadequate pipeline qualification.

risks.name: Automation Initiative Adoption Risk
risks.description: Phase 1 automation adoption at 64% creates execution risk for efficiency gains needed to compete on price. Mixed adoption threatens expected ROI and competitive response capability.

risks.name: Target A Acquisition Integration Risk
risks.description: Target A acquisition introduces integration risk including revenue stream harmonization, system integration, and potential shareholder dilution. Cultural integration challenges with acquired team.

risks.name: GDPR and Multi-Jurisdictional Data Compliance
risks.description: Operating across UK and EU requires compliance with multiple GDPR frameworks, adequacy decisions, and SCCs for cross-border transfers. AI governance frameworks emerging across EU add complexity.

risks.name: Financial Services Client Regulatory Compliance
risks.description: Financial services clients require PCI DSS, SOC 2, and sector-specific data handling protocols. Non-compliance could result in client loss and regulatory penalties.

risks.name: Territory Overlap and Sales Governance
risks.description: DACH territory overlap created internal friction requiring realignment. Inadequate sales operations governance led to channel conflict and customer confusion.

risks.name: Revenue Recognition Complexity
risks.description: Multi-element arrangements, variable consideration, and Target A integration create revenue recognition complexity under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Competitive pricing pressure may impact contract terms.

risks.name: Customer Health Monitoring Inadequacy
risks.description: Account A loss revealed inadequate early warning systems. Customer engagement occurred too late in vendor consolidation process, indicating weak account management protocols.
risks.risk_tolerance: Early warning triggers 120+ days before renewal

risks.name: Growth Deceleration and Margin Pressure
risks.description: Revenue growth declining from 8.2% to 6.8% YoY with competitive pricing pressure threatening margins. While FCF positive ($2.6M-$4.0M quarterly), trajectory concerning.

risks.name: Information Security and Client Data Protection
risks.description: IT services provider holding client data across multiple industries faces cyber threats. SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 compliance required for enterprise clients.

risks.name: AI Governance and Emerging Technology Compliance
risks.description: Automation initiatives using AI must comply with emerging EU AI governance frameworks. Risk of non-compliance as regulations evolve, particularly for client-facing automation.
risks.risk_tolerance: Compliance with all applicable AI regulations

risks.name: Executive Relationship Mapping Gaps
risks.description: Insufficient executive relationship mapping with key accounts enabled competitor displacement. Lack of C-level engagement protocols for strategic accounts.
risks.risk_tolerance: Executive engagement documented for all top 20 accounts

risks.name: Contractor and Subcontractor Quality Risk
risks.description: Delivery capacity supplemented by contractors creates quality consistency and confidentiality risks. Contractor management protocols require strengthening.

risks.name: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery
risks.description: Service delivery dependency on key systems and personnel creates business continuity risk. DR capabilities require validation for client SLA compliance.

risk_assessments.notes: Account A loss ($3.4M) absorbed in Q3. Enhanced early warning systems deployed. Top 10 concentration reduced to 42% from 45%.
Account A loss confirmed in Q3. Immediate elevation of risk score. Emergency account protection protocols initiated for top 10.
Concentration stable. Account A showing early warning signs but engagement underway. Additional monitoring for FS accounts.
Baseline assessment. Customer concentration within tolerance. No immediate concerns identified.
Infosys pressure ongoing. Value-based pricing strategy showing early results. Win rate stabilized but still below historical.
Win rates declined 3pp. Market share dropped 10bps. Competitive response plan being developed.
Risk elevated following Infosys EMEA acquisition announcement. 28% price reduction offers observed in market.
Retention initiatives showing impact. Attrition trending down from 12.8% peak. Critical skill retention bonuses effective.
Attrition reached 12.8%. Frankfurt practice losses particularly concerning. Emergency retention measures approved.
Attrition elevated but stable. Market compensation benchmarking completed. Retention plan in development.
Baseline assessment. Talent market tight but attrition within normal range. Monitoring key personnel risk.
Q4 renewal cohort improved to 92% vs 88% in Q3. New health scoring system deployed. Early warning protocols active.
Q3 renewal cohort at 88% vs 95% target. Significant deterioration requiring immediate intervention.
NRR at 104%, GRR at 92%. Below target but stable. Enhanced monitoring for at-risk accounts.
Baseline assessment. Retention metrics below industry benchmark but stable.
Q4 new logo at 95% quota following territory realignment. Pipeline qualification improvements effective.
Q3 improved to 88% quota. Territory realignment in DACH completed. Process corrections taking effect.
Q1 new logo at 82% quota. FS campaign required mid-quarter correction. Territory overlap issues in DACH.
Phase 1 adoption improved to 74%. Change management program launched. Phase 2 planning underway.
Adoption at 64%. ROI tracking implemented. User feedback identifying adoption barriers.
Phase 1 go-live. Initial adoption tracking established. Efficiency gains measurement framework deployed.
Integration on track. Revenue harmonization 80% complete. Cultural integration workshop series initiated.
Target A acquisition closed. Integration planning complete. Initial integration risks identified.
GDPR program mature. UK-EU transfer mechanisms validated. AI governance framework development initiated.
Annual GDPR assessment completed. No material gaps. SCCs updated for new transfers.
Routine assessment. Privacy program operating effectively. Monitoring EU AI Act developments.
Baseline assessment. Strong compliance posture maintained.
SOC 2 Type II renewed. PCI DSS compliance maintained. No client compliance issues reported.
Annual compliance review completed. All certifications current. FS client requirements met.
Mid-year assessment. Compliance program operating effectively.
Territory realignment complete. No Q4 conflicts reported. Governance protocols strengthened.
Territory realignment in progress. Transition period friction expected.
DACH territory overlap causing customer confusion and internal friction. Realignment approved.
Revenue recognition controls operating effectively. Target A integration accounting treatments documented.
Target A acquisition increases complexity. Integration accounting guidance engaged.
Standard assessment. Revenue recognition controls operating within tolerance.
Enhanced health scoring deployed. Executive engagement protocols strengthened. Early warning at 120+ days.
Account A loss root cause analysis complete. Gaps in early warning and executive engagement identified.
Growth at 6.8% YoY. FCF stable at $3.2M. Margin preservation initiatives underway.
Growth deceleration confirmed. 8.2% to 6.8% decline. Competitive pricing pressure impacting win rates.
Growth trajectory under review. Market conditions monitoring enhanced.
SOC 2 Type II renewed. ISO 27001 surveillance audit passed. No material security incidents.
Annual security assessment completed. Penetration testing clean. Security awareness training completed.
Mid-year assessment. Security controls operating effectively.
Baseline assessment. Strong security posture maintained.
AI governance framework development on track. EU AI Act compliance roadmap in place.
EU AI Act analysis completed. Impact assessment for automation initiatives underway.
New risk identified as automation initiatives expand. Regulatory monitoring established.
Executive engagement protocol deployed. Top 20 accounts mapped. Quarterly executive touchpoints established.
Account A loss analysis revealed relationship mapping gaps. Protocol development initiated.
Contractor qualification program effective. Quality metrics meeting targets.
Annual contractor assessment completed. No material quality issues.
Mid-year assessment. Contractor program operating effectively.
DR test completed successfully. RTO/RPO targets met. BCP reviewed and updated.
Annual DR exercise completed. Minor process improvements identified.
Mid-year assessment. BC/DR capabilities adequate. Planning for annual exercise.
Baseline assessment. BC/DR program in place and operational.

controls.name: Executive Relationship Mapping and Engagement
controls.description: Systematic mapping of C-level relationships at strategic accounts with quarterly engagement requirements and escalation protocols
controls.test_procedure: Review executive engagement logs, verify quarterly touchpoints, validate CRM data accuracy
controls.evidence_required: CRM executive activity reports, meeting minutes, relationship maps

controls.name: Customer Health Score Monitoring
controls.description: Automated health scoring system with thresholds triggering proactive engagement and escalation workflows
controls.test_procedure: Review health score calculations, validate threshold triggers, test escalation workflows
controls.evidence_required: Health score reports, escalation tickets, intervention records

controls.name: Revenue Concentration Monitoring
controls.description: Monthly analysis of customer concentration metrics with board-level reporting when thresholds exceeded
controls.test_procedure: Verify concentration calculations, review threshold alerts, confirm board reporting
controls.evidence_required: Concentration analysis reports, board materials, alert logs

controls.name: Competitive Intelligence Monitoring
controls.description: Systematic tracking of competitor activities, pricing strategies, and market positioning with weekly intelligence briefings
controls.test_procedure: Review intelligence briefings, validate source coverage, assess actionability of insights
controls.evidence_required: Intelligence briefings, competitor analysis reports, action logs

controls.name: Win/Loss Analysis Process
controls.description: Structured analysis of all significant deals won and lost with root cause identification and corrective actions
controls.test_procedure: Review win/loss reports, verify corrective action tracking, validate trend analysis
controls.evidence_required: Win/loss analysis reports, action tracking logs, trend reports

controls.name: Value-Based Pricing Governance
controls.description: Pricing approval matrix with escalation for competitive deals, ensuring margin protection while enabling competitive response
controls.test_procedure: Review pricing approvals, verify margin compliance, test escalation workflows
controls.evidence_required: Pricing approval records, margin analysis, escalation logs

controls.name: Critical Role Succession Planning

controls.name: Compensation Benchmarking and Adjustment

controls.evidence_required: Market analysis reports, adjustment approvals, retention metrics

controls.name: Flight Risk Early Warning

controls.test_procedure: Review flight risk indicators, verify intervention tracking, assess prediction accuracy
controls.evidence_required: Flight risk reports, intervention records, retention outcomes

controls.name: Net Revenue Retention Monitoring
controls.description: Weekly NRR and GRR tracking with root cause analysis for underperforming cohorts

controls.test_procedure: Review DR test plans, verify execution results, assess RTO/RPO achievement
controls.evidence_required: DR test plans, execution reports, metrics validation

control_assessments.test_evidence: Reviewed executive engagement logs for Q4. All top 20 accounts had documented quarterly touchpoints. CRM relationship maps current.
Health scoring system generating accurate scores. Threshold triggers operating correctly. 12 escalations in Q4 appropriately handled.
Intelligence briefings produced weekly. Source coverage adequate for major competitors. Some gaps in insight actionability and response tracking.
Succession plans exist for 75% of critical roles. Cross-training completion at 62%. Frankfurt practice succession gaps identified.
ROI calculations performed quarterly. Methodology sound. Efficiency gains validation challenging due to inconsistent baseline data.
Survey conducted in Q4. Engagement metrics collected. Intervention effectiveness tracking immature.
AI governance framework documented. Risk assessments incomplete for all automation use cases. EU AI Act compliance roadmap in development.
GDPR monitoring effective. No compliance gaps identified. Privacy controls operating as designed.
SOC 2 controls operating effectively. Automated evidence collection functioning. No exceptions identified.
Information security program mature. ISO 27001 aligned. No material security incidents in assessment period.
Revenue recognition policies consistently applied. Management review effective. Documentation adequate.
BCP current and comprehensive. Annual review completed. Critical functions covered with appropriate RTOs.

control_deficiencies.description: Competitive intelligence insights not consistently translated into actionable response plans. Tracking of responses to competitive moves incomplete.
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Lack of formal competitive response protocol and ownership of action tracking
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Implement formal competitive response protocol with action owner assignment and tracking system

control_deficiencies.description: Succession planning incomplete for 25% of critical roles. Cross-training completion below target at 62%. DACH region particularly underserved.
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Insufficient priority and resource allocation for succession planning program
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Accelerate succession planning completion with focus on DACH. Mandate cross-training completion for all critical roles.

control_deficiencies.description: ROI validation for automation initiatives hampered by inconsistent baseline data. Efficiency gains not always attributable to specific automation features.
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Baseline metrics not established prior to automation implementation in all cases
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Implement mandatory baseline measurement protocol for all future automation phases. Enhance measurement for existing implementations.

control_deficiencies.description: Cultural integration assessment interventions not systematically tracked for effectiveness. Limited ability to correlate interventions with engagement outcomes.
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Immature intervention tracking process for M&A integration activities
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Implement formal intervention tracking with outcome measurement linked to engagement metrics

control_deficiencies.description: AI governance framework incomplete. Risk assessments pending for multiple automation use cases. EU AI Act compliance roadmap in early stages.
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Emerging regulatory requirements and rapid automation deployment creating documentation gaps
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Complete risk assessments for all AI/ML use cases. Finalize EU AI Act compliance roadmap by Q1.

factors.notes: Account A loss demonstrates concentration vulnerability

audit_risk_assessments.notes: Elevated risk rating due to Q3 events. Prioritized for Q1 2025 audit.
Risk elevated following Account A post-mortem analysis
Talent retention concerns require audit focus
Major acquisition integration warrants audit attention
Covered by SOC 2 external audit; internal audit focus on automation
External audit coverage adequate; internal focus on integration
Strong compliance posture; monitoring AI governance development

audit_findings.title: Inadequate Early Warning Systems for At-Risk Renewals
audit_findings.criteria: Customer success processes should provide early warning of renewal risk with sufficient lead time for intervention
audit_findings.condition: Account A loss revealed that customer engagement occurred too late in the vendor consolidation process. Health scoring system deployed post-loss now shows effectiveness, but historical controls were inadequate.
audit_findings.cause: Insufficient investment in customer health monitoring technology and executive relationship mapping. Reliance on manual account management without systematic risk indicators.
audit_findings.effect: Loss of $3.4M ARR account to competitor. Defensive rather than proactive positioning. Reputational impact in financial services vertical.
audit_findings.recommendation: 1) Validate health scoring system coverage across all enterprise accounts. 2) Implement executive engagement protocol for top 20 accounts. 3) Establish 120-day minimum early warning lead time requirement.
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Health scoring system already deployed. Executive engagement protocol implementation in progress. Resources allocated for completion by target date.

audit_findings.title: Competitive Intelligence Action Gap
audit_findings.criteria: Competitive intelligence should translate into actionable response plans with tracked outcomes
audit_findings.condition: Weekly intelligence briefings produced but action items not systematically tracked. Limited ability to correlate competitive moves with response effectiveness.
audit_findings.cause: Lack of formal competitive response protocol. No clear ownership of action tracking. Intelligence function focused on collection rather than action.
audit_findings.effect: Delayed response to Infosys competitive moves. Win rate decline of 3pp before coordinated response developed.
audit_findings.recommendation: 1) Implement formal competitive response protocol. 2) Assign action ownership with tracking accountability. 3) Establish effectiveness metrics for competitive responses.
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Competitive response protocol under development. Action tracking system being implemented in Q1.

audit_findings.title: Succession Planning Coverage Gaps
audit_findings.criteria: All critical roles should have documented succession plans with qualified successors identified
audit_findings.condition: Succession plans exist for only 75% of critical roles. Cross-training completion at 62%. DACH region particularly underserved following Frankfurt practice losses.
audit_findings.cause: Insufficient priority and resource allocation. Rapid growth outpaced succession planning program development. DACH losses created unexpected gaps.
audit_findings.effect: Delivery risk for key projects. Single points of failure in critical customer relationships. Elevated retention risk for remaining critical personnel.
audit_findings.recommendation: 1) Complete succession plans for all critical roles by Q1 end. 2) Mandate cross-training completion for critical roles. 3) Prioritize DACH region succession coverage.
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Accelerated program underway. Additional HR resources allocated to succession planning.

audit_findings.title: Automation ROI Validation Weakness
audit_findings.criteria: Automation initiatives should have validated ROI with documented efficiency gains against baselines
audit_findings.condition: Phase 1 automation adoption at 64%. ROI calculations performed but efficiency gains validation challenged by inconsistent baseline data.
audit_findings.cause: Baseline metrics not established prior to automation implementation in all cases. Enthusiasm for deployment without measurement discipline.
audit_findings.effect: Inability to validate claimed efficiency gains. Risk of continued investment without proven return. Potential for inflated benefit claims.
audit_findings.recommendation: 1) Implement mandatory baseline measurement protocol for Phase 2. 2) Enhance measurement capability for Phase 1 implementations. 3) Establish independent ROI validation process.
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Baseline measurement protocol being developed. Will be mandatory for all Phase 2 implementations.

audit_findings.title: Integration Cultural Alignment Tracking
audit_findings.criteria: M&A integration should include systematic cultural alignment assessment with intervention tracking
audit_findings.condition: Cultural integration surveys conducted but intervention effectiveness not systematically tracked. Limited ability to correlate interventions with engagement outcomes.
audit_findings.cause: Immature integration playbook for cultural aspects. HR processes focused on compliance rather than cultural integration. First major acquisition in 3 years.
audit_findings.effect: Risk of cultural misalignment reducing integration success. Potential for acquired talent attrition. Suboptimal realization of acquisition value.
audit_findings.recommendation: 1) Implement formal intervention tracking with outcome measurement. 2) Link interventions to engagement metrics. 3) Establish cultural integration dashboard for leadership visibility.
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Integration team working with HR to implement intervention tracking. Dashboard development underway.

audit_findings.title: AI Governance Framework Maturity
audit_findings.criteria: AI/ML implementations should have documented governance framework with risk assessments aligned to emerging regulations
audit_findings.condition: AI governance framework documented but incomplete. Risk assessments pending for multiple automation use cases. EU AI Act compliance roadmap in early stages.
audit_findings.cause: Rapid automation deployment outpacing governance development. Emerging regulations creating moving target. Resource constraints in compliance function.
audit_findings.effect: Potential regulatory non-compliance as EU AI Act becomes effective. Reputational risk from inadequate AI governance. Client confidence concerns.
audit_findings.recommendation: 1) Complete risk assessments for all AI/ML use cases by Q1 end. 2) Finalize EU AI Act compliance roadmap. 3) Establish AI governance committee with regular oversight.
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. AI governance workstream established. External counsel engaged for EU AI Act analysis.

assessment_methods.name: Process Walkthrough
assessment_methods.description: Structured walkthrough of process from initiation to completion with process owner, documenting controls, handoffs, and system interactions
procedure_steps: Schedule walkthrough with process owner and key participants
Document process flow and control points
Identify inputs, outputs, and system interactions
Test sample transactions through the process

assessment_methods.name: Sample Testing
assessment_methods.description: Selection and testing of sample transactions to validate control operation and effectiveness
procedure_steps: Test each sample item against control criteria

assessment_methods.name: Data Analytics
assessment_methods.description: Use of data analytics tools to test full populations for anomalies and control exceptions
procedure_steps: Obtain complete data extract from source systems
Apply analytics procedures to identify anomalies
Summarize findings with root cause analysis

assessment_methods.name: Document Review
assessment_methods.description: Examination of policies, procedures, and supporting documentation for adequacy and currency

assessment_methods.name: System Access Review
assessment_methods.description: Review of system access rights and segregation of duties for appropriateness
procedure_steps: Obtain user access reports from target systems

assessment_methods.name: Interview and Inquiry
assessment_methods.description: Structured interviews with process owners, control operators, and stakeholders
procedure_steps: Develop interview guide based on audit objectives

assessment_methods.name: Reperformance
assessment_methods.description: Independent reperformance of control procedures to validate operating effectiveness
procedure_steps: Compare results to control owner execution

assessment_methods.name: Observation
assessment_methods.description: Direct observation of control operation in practice
procedure_steps: Schedule observation session with control owner
Note any deviations from documented procedures
